description: Minor vibration increase during Flight 8. Amplitude 0.15g, within nominal range.
root_cause: Dust ingestion in rotor hub. Self-cleared after 2 subsequent flights.
resolution: No action required. Added to monitoring trend.

description: Battery voltage drop faster than predicted during Flight 15.
root_cause: Low ambient temperature (-62°C) reduced battery capacity. Thermal model did not account for early morning flight.
resolution: Updated thermal model. Added pre-flight battery pre-heat requirement for flights below -55°C.

description: Upper rotor bearing degradation detected via vibration signature increase. Progressive over 5 flights.
root_cause: Bearing lubricant degradation from thermal cycling beyond design life. Mars dust contamination accelerated wear.
resolution: Bearing replacement procedure developed but not executed (end of mission). Key learning: bearing life on Mars approximately 60% of Earth-tested life.

description: Nav camera image quality degraded during Flight 22. Autonomous landing accuracy reduced.
root_cause: Dust deposition on camera lens. Accumulated over 10 flights without cleaning.
resolution: Lens cleaned. Added camera lens cleaning to pre-flight checklist every 5 flights.

description: Telemetry dropout for 45 seconds during Flight 27.
root_cause: Terrain shadowing during low-altitude flight in crater. Known comm dead zone.
resolution: Updated flight planning to include comm link margin analysis. No hardware issue.

description: Blade tip damage detected after high-wind flight. 2mm chip on Blade 1 trailing edge.
root_cause: FOD impact (small rock lofted by rotor downwash on sandy terrain).
resolution: Flight altitude restrictions imposed for sandy terrain. Blade damage within structural margin — continued flying.

description: Flight 38 vibration amplitude reached 0.19g, near alert threshold. Transient event, returned to normal.
root_cause: Under investigation at time. Bearing inspection (MA-008) found bearing play approaching upper spec limit.
resolution: Alert threshold lowered. Bearing replacement recommended. Monitoring increased to every-flight analysis.

description: Right-front wheel motor current spike during traverse over rocky terrain.
root_cause: Mechanical obstruction (wedged rock) in wheel drive train. Cleared by reverse driving.
resolution: Added wheel current monitoring to autonomous fault protection. Terrain avoidance algorithm updated.

description: Flight control software watchdog timer triggered during pre-flight checks. Flight delayed.
root_cause: Memory leak in telemetry logging module. Accumulated over 30 flights without reboot.
resolution: Patched in v2.2.0. Added periodic memory cleanup. Mandatory reboot every 20 flights.

description: Catastrophic bearing failure during Earth-based qualification testing at -40°C. Rotor assembly seized.
root_cause: Lubricant crystallization at extreme cold. Test bearing was from early production lot with incorrect lubricant specification.
resolution: Lubricant specification updated to MIL-PRF-27617 Type III. All flight bearings re-screened. MSRH uses corrected specification.

description: Solar panel output 5% below predicted after dust storm.
root_cause: Dust deposition from regional dust event (tau 1.8).
resolution: Panel cleaning performed (MA-004 follow-up). Output restored.

description: Sample gripper failed to release sample tube on first attempt.
root_cause: Fine dust contamination in gripper mechanism. Increased friction in release actuator.
resolution: Gripper mechanism cleaned. Added dust purge cycle to pre-release sequence.

description: Gradual vibration increase correlated with rotor hour accumulation. Pattern matched bearing wear signature.
root_cause: Normal bearing wear accelerated by Mars dust environment. Vibration increase was predictable from bearing play measurements.
resolution: Developed predictive model: vibration_amplitude = f(bearing_play, rotor_hours, dust_exposure). Model accuracy ±8%.

description: Motor temperature 4°C above prediction during Flight 42 (extended duration flight).
root_cause: Higher-than-expected aerodynamic load from headwind. Motor worked harder, generating more heat.
resolution: No action — within thermal margin. Updated thermal model with wind load factor.

description: Flight 47: vibration amplitude reached 0.22g in final 30 seconds of flight, exceeding 0.186g threshold by 18%. Auto-grounding triggered.
root_cause: UNDER INVESTIGATION. Preliminary assessment: consistent with progressive bearing degradation (see ANM-003, ANM-007, ANM-013 heritage pattern). Bearing play was 0.0035mm at last inspection (MA-010), spec limit is 0.004mm.
resolution: PENDING. Helicopter grounded. Anomaly review board convened.
